user_query:
I want to add a new skill to my agent that generates consistent, no-blame incident postmortems. Please create a fully-structured SKILL.md for it, following the skill anatomy with YAML frontmatter and all mandatory sections. Read input/answers.json for the 5 interview answers and input/metadata.json for the metadata. Name the skill incident-postmortem-writer (kebab-case). Requirements:
- Save the SKILL.md to output/skills/incident-postmortem-writer/SKILL.md.
- The YAML frontmatter must include: name, a single-line description under 200 characters that includes trigger phrases from the input (e.g., "create a postmortem", "incident report", or "RCA template"), and metadata as single-line JSON containing the provided emoji, homepage, and os fields.
- Include all mandatory sections: WHEN TO TRIGGER, WHEN NOT TO TRIGGER, PREREQUISITES, PROTOCOL/PROCESS, SECURITY CONSIDERATIONS, RULES, and OUTPUT FORMAT.
- PROTOCOL/PROCESS must be a numbered sequence with at least 6 concrete steps that an agent can follow end-to-end.
- SECURITY CONSIDERATIONS must explicitly mention what is read/written, and the limits around "network" and "credentials".
- RULES must be a numbered list with at least 5 items; Rule 1 must explicitly include the phrase "no-blame".
- OUTPUT FORMAT must provide a ready-to-use Markdown template for a postmortem with sections for Title, Summary, Impact, Timeline, Root Cause (5 Whys), Contributing Factors, Detection, Response, What Went Well, What Went Wrong, and Action Items (include item fields: description, owner, due_date, status).
- Validate your work and write a validation report to output/skills/incident-postmortem-writer/VALIDATION.json with boolean fields: {"yaml_parses": true/false, "metadata_single_line": true/false, "description_under_200": true/false, "has_sections": true/false, "protocol_steps_count": number, "first_rule_has_no_blame": true/false, "security_mentions_network_and_credentials": true/false, "output_template_includes_required_sections": true/false} and include an "errors" array of strings if any checks fail.
- If anything in the inputs is missing or ambiguous, list your assumptions and open questions in output/skills/incident-postmortem-writer/gaps-and-assumptions.md with two headings: "Assumptions" and "Open Questions".
Make sure all output paths are relative as shown and only write under output/.
